spartan.title: Spartan Biped
spartan.desc: A Spartan biped. Use a variant ID to control the armor       permutation.

elite.title: Elite Biped
elite.desc: An Elite biped. Use a variant ID to control the armor       permutation.

monitor.title: Monitor Biped
monitor.desc: A Monitor biped. It spawns with a default weapon that behaves exactly like a Focus       Rifle, but is invisible.

flag.title: Flag
flag.desc: A flag. It can be picked up by a player, carried, and used as a melee weapon.

bomb.title: Bomb
bomb.desc: A UNSC bomb. It can be picked up by a player, carried, and used as a melee weapon.

skull.title: Skull
skull.desc: A skull. It can be picked up by a player, carried, and used as a melee weapon.

hill_marker.title: Hill Marker
hill_marker.desc: An invisible hill marker, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

flag_stand.title: Flag Stand
flag_stand.desc: A flag stand. It is not solid.

capture_plate.title: Capture Plate
capture_plate.desc: A thick, olive drab plate of metal. It is not solid. It is typically used as a spawn or       arming point for bombs.

frag_grenade.title: Frag Grenade
frag_grenade.desc: A fragmentation grenade that detonates shortly after bouncing off of a single surface.

plasma_grenade.title: Plasma Grenade
plasma_grenade.desc: A plasma grenade that sticks to targets.

spike_grenade.title: Spike Grenade
spike_grenade.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. A development leftover from Halo 3: a spike grenade similar in design to a Stielhandgranate,       which sticks to targets when thrown and unleashes a hail of spikes when detonating.

firebomb_grenade.title: Firebomb Grenade
firebomb_grenade.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. A development leftover from Halo 3: a firebomb which shatters upon impact and ignites the       ground at the impact site.

dmr.title: DMR
dmr.desc: A designated marksman rifle: the main precision weapon in Halo: Reach.       Headshot-capable, with a scope for long-range combat and a fire rate appropriate for       medium-range combat. It can also damage vehicles, though at the cost of a large amount       of ammo.

plasma_pistol.title: Plasma Pistol
plasma_pistol.desc: A small Covenant pistol that fires green plasma bolts. It can be charged up to fire a       very large plasma bolt accompanied by an EMP that disables vehicles and drains player       shields.

spiker.title: Spiker
spiker.desc: An automatic weapon introduced in Halo 3, created by the Brutes. It fires spikes that       arc over a short distance due to gravity.

smg.title: SMG
smg.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. A development leftover from Halo 2: a fully-automatic submachine gun with a high fire       rate and very low accuracy.

needle_rifle.title: Needle Rifle
needle_rifle.desc: The Covenant counterpart to the DMR. Needle Rifles are capable of triggering deadly       "supercombine" explosions if three shots hit an unshielded target. Unlike the DMR, however,       they do effectively no damage to vehicles.

plasma_repeater.title: Plasma Repeater
plasma_repeater.desc: The Covenant counterpart to the Assault Rifle. Unlike most Covenant weapons, the Plasma       Repeater's cooldown can be initiated manually, akin to early reloading when using a human       weapon.

energy_sword.title: Energy Sword
energy_sword.desc: A Covenant melee weapon which generates a blade made of plasma. If Title Update 1       settings are enabled, then the only thing that can block a blow from an Energy Sword       is another Energy Sword; if the relevant settings are not enabled, then any melee       attack can block a sword swing.

magnum.title: Magnum
magnum.desc: A large pistol designed for use by Spartans. It has a relatively fast fire rate       compared to its Halo 3 counterpart, and it has a scope.

needler.title: Needler
needler.desc: A Covenant weapon which fires a series of needles that home in on their target.       If enough needles enter an unshielded target, they will produce a deadly "supercombine"       explosion.

plasma_rifle.title: Plasma Rifle
plasma_rifle.desc: A fully-automatic Covenant weapon which fires plasma bolts.

rocket_launcher.title: Rocket Launcher
rocket_launcher.desc: A rocket launcher. It can load up to two rockets at a time.

sniper_rifle.title: Sniper Rifle
sniper_rifle.desc: A human sniper rifle. It has two levels of zoom, and can load four rounds at a time.

brute_shot.title: <del>Beam</del> <ins>Focus</ins> Rifle
brute_shot.desc: This value seems to have been intended for Beam Rifles, but now it refers to Focus Rifles.

spartan_laser.title: Spartan Laser
spartan_laser.desc: A human weapon: a massive particle beam cannon which can fire four rounds. It has a long       charge-up time, and its laser sight can give away its user's position and intent.

gravity_hammer.title: Gravity Hammer
gravity_hammer.desc: A Covenant melee weapon which generates a powerful physics impuolse when swung.

mauler.title: Warthog
mauler.desc: A Warthog. Use a variant ID to select a specific variety       of Warthog to spawn.

ghost.title: Ghost
ghost.desc: A single-occupant Covenant vehicle capable of strafing and rapid boosting. It has two       mounted plasma cannons.

scorpion.title: Scorpion
scorpion.desc: A tank, with a main cannon operable by the driver and an anti-infantry chaingun       operable by a second occupant. I hear it beats everything.

wraith.title: Wraith
wraith.desc: A Covenant tank, with a mortar launcher operable by the driver and an anti-infantry       plasma turret operable by a second occupant.

banshee.title: Banshee
banshee.desc: A single-occupant Covenant air vehicle equipped with a plasma cannon and a fuel rod       bomb launcher.

mongoose.title: Mongoose
mongoose.desc: A two-seater ATV with no weapons.

chopper.title: Falcon
chopper.desc: A human air vehicle with two door guns operable by passengers.

sabre.title: Sprint
sabre.desc: An Armor Ability that allows its user to sprint forward for short periods of time.

jetpack.title: Jetpack
jetpack.desc: An Armor Ability that allows its user to hover upwards for short periods of time.

armor_lock.title: Armor Lock
armor_lock.desc: An Armor Ability that allows players to become completely invincible to all damage,       reflect incoming projectiles including actual rockets fired from an actual rocket       launcher directly back at the person firing them, for several seconds, while also       releasing an EMP upon deactivation which disables vehicles and strips shields, all at       the minor cost of immobilizing the user (but not their teammates) while activated.

power_fist.title: Active Camo (Armor Ability
power_fist.desc: An Armor Ability that allows its user to become invisible for short periods of       time. The camouflage effect becomes significantly weaker if the user moves, and the       ability also emits a radar jamming effect that can alert enemies to its use.

ammo_pack.title: Revenant
ammo_pack.desc: A Covenant vehicle that can move like a Ghost, but has a miniaturized mortar launcher akin       to a Wraith and a passenger seat akin to a Mongoose.

pickup_truck.title: Pickup Truck
pickup_truck.desc: A truck with two front seats and a large truck bed. This is the first land vehicle seen in Reach's campaign, in the Winter Contingency mission.

focus_rifle.title: Focus Rifle
focus_rifle.desc: A Covenant weapon that fires a weak particle beam.

territory_static.title: Respawn Zone
territory_static.desc: A respawn zone marker, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

invasion_elite_buy.title: Plasma Launcher
invasion_elite_buy.desc: A Covenant weapon which can lock onto targets and fire up to four armed plasma grenades       after charging up. These grenades home in on targets.

fusion_coil.title: Fusion Coil
fusion_coil.desc: A small human power source, shaped like a rectangular prism, sized 0.25 x 0.25 x 0.5 Forge       units. It is explosive and will detonate when it receives too much damage.

unsc_shield_generator.title: Initial Spawn Point
unsc_shield_generator.desc: A respawn point that is only used to spawn players in for the first time, at the start of       a round.

req_unsc_laser.title: Respawn Zone, Fireteam 1
req_unsc_laser.desc: A special type of Respawn Zone defined on Boneyard. It can be seen in Forge on maps like Boneyard, but not edited.

fireteam_2_respawn_zone.title: Respawn Zone, Fireteam 2
fireteam_2_respawn_zone.desc: A special type of Respawn Zone defined on Boneyard. It can be seen in Forge on maps like Boneyard, but not edited.

fireteam_3_respawn_zone.title: Respawn Zone, Fireteam 3
fireteam_3_respawn_zone.desc: A special type of Respawn Zone defined on Boneyard. It can be seen in Forge on maps like Boneyard, but not edited.

fireteam_4_respawn_zone.title: Respawn Zone, Fireteam 4
fireteam_4_respawn_zone.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. As of this writing, no multiplayer maps define this tag; fireteam-specific respawn zones only go up to 3.

semi_truck.title: Semi Truck
semi_truck.desc: A semi truck with no trailer attached. The ones placeable in Forge with the Thorage       update have a truck bed attached, but the ones spawned in Megalo don't by default; as of       this writing, 343 Industries would have to add a new variant ID for Megalo in order to       make the truck bed available to scripts.

soccer_ball.title: Soccer Ball
soccer_ball.desc: A giant soccer ball, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

golf_ball.title: Golf Ball
golf_ball.desc: A giant golf ball, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

golf_ball_blue.title: Golf Ball, Blue
golf_ball_blue.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object.

golf_ball_red.title: Golf Ball, Red
golf_ball_red.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object.

golf_club.title: Golf Club
golf_club.desc: A reskinned Gravity Hammer which looks like a gigantic golf club.

golf_cup.title: Golf Cup
golf_cup.desc: A golf cup with a holographic blue flag, surrounded by a circular patch of astroturf. The       object has peculiar physics, with a magnetism effect near the cup; this is intended to help       pull golf balls into the cup, but it also affects some other objects, including Mongooses.

golf_tee.title: Dice
golf_tee.desc: A giant six-sided die, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. It is a muted green       with white dots. The pivot point is on the "six" face, but is slightly off-center.

covenant_crate.title: Covenant Crate
covenant_crate.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. Defined on some Firefight maps, including Overlook. It is a Covenant weapon       crate. This same crate is available on some Multiplayer maps, including Forge       World, but it uses a different tag, and Megalo is not set up to make that tag       available to scripts.

eradicator_loadout.title: Flare
eradicator_loadout.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. An unused scenery item; it is not a leftover of the Flare equipment item from Halo 3.

glow_stick.title: Glowstick
glow_stick.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. Defined on some Firefight maps, but doesn't appear to be defined in any Multiplayer maps.

concussion_rifle.title: Concussion Rifle
concussion_rifle.desc: A Covenant weapon similar to the Brute Shot; it does less damage but has a greater physics       impulse.

grenade_launcher.title: Grenade Launcher
grenade_launcher.desc: A grenade launcher. Holding down the trigger delays the detonation of a fired grenade,       and causes the grenade to release an EMP when it detonates.

phantom_approach.title: phantom_approach
phantom_approach.desc: A device machine with the Phantom model, suitable for scripted animation.

hologram.title: Hologram
hologram.desc: An Armor Ability that sends out an illusory copy of the user. The copy "dies" after a short       delay or if it takes too much damage.

evade.title: Evade
evade.desc: An Armor Ability that allows the user to perform high-speed dodge rolls.

unsc_data_core.title: UNSC Data Core
unsc_data_core.desc: A UNSC data core, used as the "capture the flag" objective in Invasion: Boneyard. It can be       picked up and carried by a player, reducing their speed and preventing them from attacking.

danger_zone.title: Danger Zone
danger_zone.desc: Analysis of this object's map tag is inconclusive but suggests that it influences spawning       when a shape is set on it.

teleporter_sender.title: Phantom, Overwatch
teleporter_sender.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. A device machine with the Phantom model, suitable for scripted animation.

longsword.title: Longsword
longsword.desc: A relatively low-detail static model of a Longsword bomber. The tag is defined on Boneyard.

particle_emitter_fire.title: Particle Emitter, Fire
particle_emitter_fire.desc: An invisible object which emits fire particles. This is purely a graphical effect and does no       damage on its own.

phantom.title: Phantom, Vehicle
phantom.desc: A drivable Phantom. Cannot be driven by default in an unmodified map due to seat and/or animation limitations.

pelican.title: Pelican
pelican.desc: A pelican device machine suitible for scripted animations. Appears to only be spawnable on Spire.

armory_shelf.title:
i.e. flat 
            grey
armory_shelf.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object.

respawn_zone_weak.title: Respawn Zone, Weak
respawn_zone_weak.desc: A respawn zone with a weak influence, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

respawn_zone_weak_anti.title: Respawn Zone, Weak, Anti
respawn_zone_weak_anti.desc: A respawn zone with a weak negative influence, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

resupply_capsule.title: Initial Loadout Camera
resupply_capsule.desc: An object which defines a camera view that can be used during the initial loadout selection       period at the start of a round. Note that the game doesn't anchor the camera to one of       these markers when using it; it simply moves the camera to it. The distinction being       made is that using scripts to move an Initial Loadout Camera marker will not animate the camera       view being displayed.

invisible_covenant_resupply_capsule.title: invis_cov_resupply_capsule
invisible_covenant_resupply_capsule.desc: This tag is defined on Boneyard, and inspection with third-party tools indicates that it takes       the form of an invisible cube. Its function has yet to be tested in-game.

covenant_power_core.title: Covenant Power Core
covenant_power_core.desc: A Covenant power core, used as the "capture the flag" objective in Invasion: Spire. It can be       picked up and carried by a player, reducing their speed and preventing them from attacking.

fuel_rod_gun.title: Fuel Rod Gun
fuel_rod_gun.desc: A Covenant heavy weapon that fires explosive fuel rods, which may bounce once depending on       the angle at which they hit a surface.

dropzone_boundary.title: dropzone_boundary
dropzone_boundary.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object.

shield_door_small.title: Shield Door, Small
shield_door_small.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. Defined on some Firefight maps, including Courtyard.

shield_door_medium.title: Shield Door, Medium
shield_door_medium.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. Defined on some Firefight maps, including Courtyard.

shield_door_large.title: Shield Door, Large
shield_door_large.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object. Defined on some Firefight maps, including Courtyard.

drop_shield.title: Drop Shield
drop_shield.desc: An Armor Ability which allows the user to place a small bubble shield at their location.

detached_machine_gun_turret.title: Machine Gun Turret, Detached
detached_machine_gun_turret.desc: An infantry-operable turret that has been detached from its stand. It has a finite ammo       supply and cannot be refilled. It can be carried in addition to a primary and secondary weapon,       but will slow its user's movement.

machine_gun_turret.title: Machine Gun Turret
machine_gun_turret.desc: An infantry-operable turret mounted on a stand. While mounted, the turret has unlimited ammo       but cannot be moved; if the stand is broken, the turret can be carried but will have a finite       ammo supply.

detached_plasma_cannon.title: Plasma Cannon, Detached
detached_plasma_cannon.desc: An infantry-operable turret that has been detached from its stand. It has a finite ammo       supply and cannot be refilled. It can be carried in addition to a primary and secondary weapon,       but will slow its user's movement.

plasma_cannon.title: Plasma Cannon
plasma_cannon.desc: An infantry-operable turret mounted on a stand. While mounted, the turret has unlimited ammo       but cannot be moved; if the stand is broken, the turret can be carried but will have a finite       ammo supply.

cargo_truck.title: Cargo Truck
cargo_truck.desc: This value is not used by the engine and will not spawn or match any object.

electric_cart.title: Electric Cart
electric_cart.desc: An electric cart with no armaments.

military_truck.title: ONI Van
military_truck.desc: A huge black van with a driver and passenger seat. It is fairly slow.

warthog_turret.title: Warthog Turret, Normal
warthog_turret.desc: A Warthog rear chaingun, separate from the Warthog itself.

warthog_turret_gauss.title: Warthog Turret, Gauss
warthog_turret_gauss.desc: A Warthog rear gauss turret, separate from the Warthog itself.

warthog_turret_rocket.title: Warthog Turret, Rocket
warthog_turret_rocket.desc: A Warthog rear rocket turret, separate from the Warthog itself.

scorpion_turret_anti_infantry.title: Scorpion Turret, Chaingun
scorpion_turret_anti_infantry.desc: A Scorpion's anti-infantry chaingun, separate from the Scorpion itself.

falcon_turret_grenade_left.title: Falcon Turret, Grenade Left
falcon_turret_grenade_left.desc: A Falcon's left-side door-gun grenade launcher, separate from the Falcon itself.

falcon_turret_grenade_right.title: Falcon Turret, Grenade Right
falcon_turret_grenade_right.desc: A Falcon's right-side door-gun grenade launcher, separate from the Falcon itself.

wraith_turret_anti_infantry.title: Wraith Turret, Anti-Infantry
wraith_turret_anti_infantry.desc: A Wraith's anti-infantry plasma turret, separate from the Wraith itself.

target_locator.title: Target Locator
target_locator.desc: A Target Locator capable of calling down a devastating air strike. This weapon will only spawn       on maps that define its tag.

kill_boundary_3d_model.title: Soft
kill_boundary_3d_model.desc: A Block 1x1 Flat, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

shade_gun_anti_air.title: Shade Turret Gun, Anti-Air
shade_gun_anti_air.desc: The "gun" part of a Shade turret; the base is not included. This variant fires devastatingly       powerful shots at a high rate. Shields protect its operator.

shade_gun_fuel_rod.title: Shade Turret Gun, Fuel Rod
shade_gun_fuel_rod.desc: The "gun" part of a Shade turret; the base is not included. This variant fires fuel rods.

shade_gun_plasma.title: Shade Turret Gun, Plasma
shade_gun_plasma.desc: The "gun" part of a Shade turret; the base is not included.

kill_ball.title: Kill Ball
kill_ball.desc: A Kill Ball, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

light_red.title: Forge Light (Red
light_red.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_blue.title: Forge Light (Blue
light_blue.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_green.title: Forge Light (Green
light_green.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_orange.title: Forge Light (Orange
light_orange.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_purple.title: Forge Light (Purple
light_purple.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_yellow.title: Forge Light (Yellow
light_yellow.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_white.title: Forge Light (White
light_white.desc: A light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Scaling this object will       resize the visible "ball" of light, but will not change the brightness or radius of the emitted       light.

light_red_flashing.title: Forge Light (Flashing Red
light_red_flashing.desc: A flashing light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

light_yellow_flashing.title: Forge Light (Flashing Yellow
light_yellow_flashing.desc: A flashing light emitter, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_colorblind.title: FX (Colorblind
fx_colorblind.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_gloomy.title: FX (Gloomy
fx_gloomy.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_juicy.title: FX (Juicy
fx_juicy.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_nova.title: FX (Nova
fx_nova.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_olde_timey.title: FX (Olde Timey
fx_olde_timey.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_pen_and_ink.title: FX (Pen And Ink
fx_pen_and_ink.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_purple.title: FX (Purple
fx_purple.desc: A Forge filter that covers the screen in a deep purple overlay, similar to the lighting in       the Nightfall mission.

fx_orange.title: FX (Orange
fx_orange.desc: A screen FX object, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.

fx_green.title: FX (Green
fx_green.desc: A Forge filter that covers the screen in a grimy, green overlay, with small visual distortions       away from the center. This filter affects the UI, and it can stack; spawning too many of it will       blind all players.

grid.title: Grid
grid.desc: A glowing blue platform with a grid texture, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge.       The platform is square, and each side is 30 Forge units long; on Tempest, a single grid placed at       the center of the map can reach from one base's frontmost man cannon to the other base's frontmost       man cannon.

sound_emitter_alarm_1.title: Invisible Cube of Alarming 1
sound_emitter_alarm_1.desc: An invisible object which emits an alarm sound. The emitter may be silent by default; call       set_scenario_interpolator_state(1, 1) to make it audible, and       set_scenario_interpolator_state(1, 0) to silence it again.

sound_emitter_alarm_2.title: Invisible Cube of Alarming 2
sound_emitter_alarm_2.desc: An invisible object which emits an alarm sound. The emitter may be silent by default; call       set_scenario_interpolator_state(2, 1) to make it audible, and       set_scenario_interpolator_state(2, 0) to silence it again.

safe_boundary.title: Safe Boundary
safe_boundary.desc: A Safe Boundary, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Players who remain outside       of all extant Safe Boundaries will be killed.

soft_safe_boundary.title: Soft Safe Boundary
soft_safe_boundary.desc: A Soft Safe Boundary, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Players who remain       outside of all extant Soft Safe Boundaries for longer than ten seconds will be killed.

kill_boundary.title: Kill Boundary
kill_boundary.desc: A Kill Boundary, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Players who enter its shape       will be killed instantly.

soft_kill_boundary.title: Soft Kill Boundary
soft_kill_boundary.desc: A Soft Kill Boundary, of the same variety as those placeable in Forge. Players who remain       inside of its shape for longer than ten seconds will be killed.

unsc_data_core_holder.title: UNSC Data Core Holder
unsc_data_core_holder.desc: The Invasion: Boneyard prop which holds the UNSC Data Core used as an objective in Phase 3.

covenant_power_module_stand.title: Covenant Power Module Stand
covenant_power_module_stand.desc: The Invasion: Spire prop linked to the Phase 1 territories.

covenant_bomb.title: Covenant Bomb
covenant_bomb.desc: The Covenant bomb used in Invasion: Breakpoint. Like the UNSC bomb, it can be picked up and       carried.

heavy_barrier.title: Heavy Barrier
heavy_barrier.desc: A massive steel barricade. This object was originally only available on Breakpoint, but the Thorage update has made it usable on Forge World.

breakpoint_bomb_door.title: dlc_invasion_bomb_door
breakpoint_bomb_door.desc: A massive metal wall. It is usable on Forge World, but as of this writing I'm not sure yet whether it will be included in Thorage.
